concerns.text: FR-004 states the system MUST record steps at which success rate crosses '[deferred] of the maximum pilot score'. The spec fails to define the pilot run protocol (which variant, which seed, which benchmark) required to establish this 'maximum pilot score' baseline. Without a defined pilot, the threshold is undefined, making the primary metric (steps-to-threshold) unmeasurable and the experiment non-reproducible.

The Assumptions section lists 'WebShop benchmark DOI ()' as a placeholder. The spec must explicitly identify the canonical source (e.g., HuggingFace dataset name/commit) for all three benchmarks (HotpotQA, MATH, WebShop) to satisfy Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) and Principle VII (Benchmark Integrity). Relying on a placeholder DOI violates the requirement for verified, reproducible data sources.

User Story 2 defines a hyperparameter grid (3x2x2 = 12 variants) plus the baseline and default. FR-006 mandates 5 seeds per configuration. This implies 14 configurations * 5 seeds = 70 training runs. The spec does not address the computational feasibility of 70 runs within the 6-hour limit mentioned in SC-005, nor does it define a strategy (e.g., early stopping, reduced seeds for ablation) to handle this scope. The current spec implies an impossible workload.
